danger_signals.meaning: Player character Simba taking damage from enemies or environmental hazards

danger_signals.visual_form: Animated hostile creatures on screen (hyenas, etc.)
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring evasion or combat engagement

danger_signals.visual_form: Bottomless pits, lava lakes, water bodies, rolling boulders, falling stalactites
danger_signals.meaning: Instant-death or damage hazards that trigger loss of life

danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct black spider sprites among collectible bugs
danger_signals.meaning: Harmful collectibles that damage Simba on contact

danger_signals.visual_form: Life counter reaches zero, return to title screen or level restart
danger_signals.meaning: Loss condition triggered by depleting all available lives

safety_signals.visual_form: Environmental marker or visual transition in level
safety_signals.meaning: Safe restart point established; player can resume here after losing a life

safety_signals.visual_form: Specific bug sprites (non-black varieties) scattered in levels
safety_signals.meaning: Collectible items that replenish Simba's health meter
Ability to stun enemies or solve puzzles; renewable defensive/puzzle-solving resource
Grants continues, allowing progression restart if game is lost

safety_signals.visual_form: Transition to Timon or Pumbaa minigame stages
safety_signals.meaning: Safe, reward-focused gameplay section; opportunity to earn extra lives and continues

safety_signals.visual_form: Character sprite changes from cub to large adult lion
safety_signals.meaning: Progression milestone; enhanced combat capabilities (scratching, mauling, throws)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Lion cub sprite (first half) / Adult lion sprite (second half)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Pride Lands terrain; natural African savanna and wildlife

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Varies by level and context (Hakuna Matata, Be Prepared scenes suggest thematic enemy groupings)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appear throughout levels as obstacles; dense in certain story-beat locations

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Adult dark-maned lion sprite with distinctive scarred appearance
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appears in final confrontation; represents climactic battle in narrative

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Smaller Simba sprite; limited combat options; reliance on jumping on enemies
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player uses roar ability to stun enemies and solve puzzles; limited offensive repertoire

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Mid-to-late game / Simba grown to adulthood
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Larger adult lion sprite; enhanced visual presence on screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Access to multiple combat moves (scratching, mauling, throws); gameplay shift from evasion to engagement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Timon and Pumbaa character sprites; distinct minigame visual presentation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Shift to bug-collection and time-based challenges; reward-focused gameplay without threat of losing lives

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Level environment and theming based on film's Hakuna Matata sequence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Gameplay adapted from cut film scene; thematic enemy and obstacle design

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Level environment and theming based on film's Be Prepared musical sequence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Gameplay adapted from cut film scene; thematic enemy density and placement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Unique boss arena; Scar sprite with scarred detail; likely contrasting color or emphasized design
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enhanced enemy AI; multiple-phase combat; climactic narrative moment requiring mastery of adult combat moves

environmental_storytelling.technique: Level design based on film narrative beats
environmental_storytelling.example: Levels labeled as story events (Can't Wait to be King, Hakuna Matata, Be Prepared) that visually represent locations and scenarios from the film
Simba transitions from cub sprite to adult lion sprite, visually communicating passage of time and character growth without dialogue
Specific hazards (lava lakes, water bodies, rolling boulders, falling stalactites) suggest savanna geography and danger types thematic to Pride Lands
Different enemy creatures appear in thematically appropriate levels; hyenas and hostile wildlife populate environments matching film's antagonistic forces
Timon and Pumbaa character appearances in bonus stages visually reinforce their comic relief role and safe-haven association in narrative

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Walks and jumps; can roar to stun enemies; limited offensive options
Takes damage; health meter depletes; may lose life if hazard contact occurs
Walks and jumps with larger sprite; access to scratch, maul, and throw attacks
Engages enemies directly with multiple attack options; primary offensive paradigm shift from cub phase
Patrol or stationary placement; standard animation loops
Aggressive approach; attack animations; may be stunned by roar or combat moves
Enhanced combat AI; multi-phase behavior; narrative climax requiring full mastery of adult combat mechanics
Bug-hunting minigame logic; time-limited challenge; avoids spiders while collecting bugs
Bug-collection minigame logic; vertical falling hazards; loses if bad bug or item reaches screen bottom

notes: The Lion King (Genesis 1994) uses sprite-based visual communication tied directly to film narrative progression. Safety and danger signals are clearly delineated through health mechanics, environmental hazard design, and collectible distinction (safe bugs vs. black spiders). Faction identity is implicit rather than explicit (no faction color schemes or sigils documented); antagonism is communicated through enemy presence and sprite design rather than faction markers. World state is visually expressed through protagonist form change (cub to adult) and level theming matching film's story beats. Environmental storytelling relies on level design fidelity to film source material and hazard-type theming. Behavioral tells are primarily tied to gameplay mechanics rather than emergent NPC behavior.
